title: Erectile Dysfunction Protocol in Rats or Mice
author: Li-Ming Jin
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yikfucw

steps.title: Rats or mice are anesthetized with...
steps.instruction: Rats or mice are anesthetized with an intraperitoneal injection with 50 mg pentobarbital/kg weight.

steps.title: A midline incision was made from umbilicus to pubis
steps.instruction: A midline incision was made from umbilicus to pubis.

steps.title: The penis was denuded of skin, and the prepuce circumcised
steps.instruction: The penis was denuded of skin, and the prepuce circumcised. The testes and epididymides were repositioned into the abdomen after they were divided from their scrotal attachments.

steps.title: Bipolar silver wire electrodes were attached...
steps.instruction: Bipolar silver wire electrodes were attached unilaterally to the cavernous nerve that arises from the ipsilateral pelvic plexus situated dorsolateral to the prostate.

steps.title: Intracavernous pressures were measured (Gould Polygraph,...
steps.instruction: Intracavernous pressures were measured (Gould Polygraph, Cleveland, Ohio) with a 25-gauge needle inserted unilaterally at the base of the penis and connected to a pressure transducer.

steps.title: Penile erection was induced electrically with...
steps.instruction: Penile erection was induced electrically with a Grass S48 square wave stimulator at 1-5 volts, 16 htz for 1 min.

steps.title: Neurostimulation was performed until a 10s...
steps.instruction: Neurostimulation was performed until a 10s maximal pressure recording was achieved, but no stimulation lasted longer than 90s. At least 3 min elapsed between repeated stimulations.

steps.title: Maximum intracavernosal pressure (Max ICP) and...
steps.instruction: Maximum intracavernosal pressure (Max ICP) and total area under the ICP curve above baseline were normalized by systemic mean arterial blood pressure (MAP) and used as indices of erectile function.
